item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Whoa, rick and morty did not invent interdimentional cable??

In fact, did this SCP article invent it?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah I just really want them to play a video game on this thing

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
There were pretty good adivces given, with the OP being receptive and stating that they would work on it, yet they never altered the article again after the date of posting.

This is too bad, because the concept is good. But it should be expanded upon. Right now, and for as long as it has been here, the article itself hasn't been pushed far enough and the test log isn't as fleshed out as it could easily be.
Not only there are so many things that should have been tested, both because they would seem obvious in universe and because they would be entertaining, that the lack of content compensates for the good idea, but the test log is also currently 15% Sliders jokes.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I really like the bit about being available for staff movie night on Fridays.
The caveat that they can only use previously tested materials strikes just the right balance between "Let's see what it does when I do this!" lolfoundation and perfectly rational caution.

After all, they could get an alternate of The Terminator from a universe where all human faces have been replaced by infectious memetic kill agents.
But once they tested the DVD and found it's just a universe where "Arnold Schwarzeneger" is a juvenile otter; why not build a bit of morale?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could you add a few more tests?

item.title: Sliders
item.content:encoded: I don't know man, the joke involving the Sliders made sense. I mean, what do you think?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted, because it made me smile. Of course Sliders would be the same.

Also, personally, I wasn't bothered by the redactions. I got everything I needed to know about this from what you gave me.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Wow great job. The censoring was just right with the "Hero of Racial Purity" statue. ;) More experiments!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Needs fewer expungements. No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I agree with others who say it needs fewer expungements, but overall I like the alternate reality TV tests. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
My only issue, and I swear I hate to nitpick, is the Schindler’s List bit. To me, I feel like The Foundation would of done a second test on it to confirm that it needs a TV to work, or at least for the records sake. Other than that, it's definitely interesting.

Perhaps a collaboration page on different media in tests would be a nice addition? I see a lot of recommendations in the comments.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
But I don't think the Foundation subscribes to that theory […]

Depends on who you ask.

The censorship in this document is extremely gratuitous, yes, but it hardly has anything to do with that particular element. This is a criticism I had of the draft when it was first posted, and that has not changed. You did remind me to downvote this, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I can't really say anything that hasn't already been said, but it still needs to be said. When I saw all the blackboxes and "[DATA EXPUNGED]"s, I assumed they were references to historical or mythical whatevers that I, living under a rock, had no way of being aware of. Reading the stuff in the comments section indicates that I was wrong to assume this. So, basically, what everyone else has said: Take out the expungements, and also give some real evidence that these videos or whatever are actually from alternate realities and not just stuff being made up.

(…Of course, if, like me, you're a firm believer in the multiverse theory, then there's no such thing as "made up" - everything is from an alternate reality. But I don't think the Foundation subscribes to that theory, so, yeah…do something about that. :P )

No vote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definitely needs less expunging

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Recording of children playing at [REDACTED] Park. (Confiscated from Agent ███)"

All of my wat.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Definite potential, but the test logs are very weak. Alternate realities need some interesting hooks, but this is mostly "And stuff was randomly a little bit different".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Except for the less redacteds and expungeds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Duke Nukem Forever.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's funny because Sliders is about alternate realities.

Sort of meta.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I personally like the Sliders bit. What it needs for testing:
-some video game
-some porn
-some medical/scientific movies (i doubt that the Foundation would miss the oportunity to know how different realities have developed. They could be for example more or differently developed in terms of medicine, having the cure for cancer but dying from a simple flu)

Test it more, I'll upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For me, this is the kind of item that makes or breaks based on the test logs, and in this case, it broke. Not enough of them, not enough detail, and what there was didn't give me any real sense that I was getting a glimpse of an alternate reality. Plus, the Sliders thing was just…meh.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What about alternate pressings of the same DVD? Studios often release alternate versions of movies - Director's cuts, special editions, or just later copies that have minor edits here or there.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You mean Sliders? A television show about travel to alternate worlds.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I don't get the slides reference, care to help anyone?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah… I rather like the general concept, but there are so many silly [REDACTED] snips that it's hard to really enjoy it. There seems to be a serious issue with people deciding to Expunge random interesting, but harmless information. :[

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is not bad, but it's too detail-lite for me to upvote. Needs a bit more information, and a whole lot less [DATA EXPUNGED].

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Needs:

- Battlestar Galactica (RDM, not TOS)

- Skyrim

- Mass Effect/Mass Effect 2

And less redacteds and expungements, especially on my three requests.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Agreed. Also think it needs more tests.

Try Skyrim!

Also, definitely less censoring in the test log!

I wanna know who the "father of racial purity" is. It has too many letters for Hitler.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, it doesn't seem too "out there" to me for scientists to measure something to the millimeter - I might end up changing it to "Approx. 6m" though.

As for the experiments, I have a few ideas for what would happen if a splitter got used, I'm working out which one might be better.

And Star Wars is one of the ones I plan on adding - probably in a "bulk change" once I get a few more too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
…a coaxial audio-visual cable measuring 6.096m in length..

They measured it to the millimeter?

Still, I like it. My experiments would would use a splitter to try to record while it's hooked up to a TV, to a T.V. with a "recordable output" (they have to make one somewhere!) Also - I don't think live broadcasts and cameras were mentioned.

AlsoI'd try it with something like all the Star Wars movie (there's what, a dozen different version of it?) to observer for any correlation.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
No, no, I got it.

E.T. Game of the Year edition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Action 52: The "Angry Video Game Nerd Approved" Edition?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sounded to me like it was just the Foundation being dicks and confiscating video of his kids he watched on duty one too many times.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's called a monument if construction starts during the person's lifetime and a memorial if it isn't, at least as I understand it. You know, because a memorial is "in memory of" the person.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I'm sure it'll all get cleared up at the disciplinary hearing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What was Agent ████ doing with that DVD?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: But it's pointy at the top. Ouch!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well, what better than a giant phallic symbol for the Father of His Country?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's because the Lincoln Memorial is fully dedicated to Lincoln and his work, whereas the Washington Monument serves the purpose of being tall.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The monuments are the "Washington Monument" and the "Lincoln Memorial", not the "Washington Memorial" and the "Lincoln Memorial". Why? I dunno.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the concept, but dislike the execution. Expungement is excessive, you could use ██████ instead of [REDACTED] in statues tests, other redactions should be removed completely. Sliders test is a good joke, but don't mesh well with the rest of the article. Inability to record anomalous output directly is pointless, since we can just recod the screen, so it can be removed too. Assumption that those videos come from alternative universes is not based on anything, for all we know the cable just creates them on the spot for it's own amusement.

No vote for now. Do something, remove all the exungements at least, and I'll upvote. If it remains as is for long, say, a month, I'll come back and downvote it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Perhaps proto-Chinese would work? There's work suggesting that the ancestor of many Chinese languages was non-tonal and morphologically complex. See works by Baxter et al. for more info.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alone in the Dark: The Good Version

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: What happens if you plug in a video game console?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: remove most of the exspongements, i'll upvote.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I, too, like the last entry in the addendum. However you could be more precise regarding to greetings written in "Old Chinese".

As a native Taiwanese who learn how to speak and write traditional Chinese fluently I have some suggestions for you:

As far as I know the term "Old Chinese" covers a very long period of time from late Shang Dynasty Oracle bone script (which I can hardly read at all) to Qin Dynasty Small Seal Script (which I can read probably 50 to 60% of its content).

Maybe you could add more details about that.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The Sliders entry was really more there as a bit of a joke - if you don't like that rationale, then just pretend that the alternate reality for that one had essentially the same show (probably a better explanation than "joke" anyway).

I have a few more ideas for log entries. I'll probably add them in later as well as give the censoring another look - probably tomorrow if it's still doing decently well.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I like the last entry in the addendum, the proto-Minoan part especially. I dislike the one above it; Sliders would be different, since the characters' reactions to different dimensions would be different than the original one. Maybe a version of Sliders with Kromaggs sliding to dystopian human-controlled planets.

No vote for now, but definitely not bad. You might want to cut down on the expungement, though.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that there are too many [REDACTED] and [DATA EXPUNGED]'s in here. The log seems interesting, but I don't think it's fleshed out enough.
